findings.message: D2 lists direct inner attributes like `#![doc(hidden)]` as required detections, but then says the judgment target is any `syn item`. A crate/file-level inner attribute lives on `syn::File::attrs`, not on an item, so an implementation that follows the item-only wording can miss the exact inner-attribute form the ADR says is in scope. The plan should explicitly include file-level attrs as scan targets, or narrow the inner-attribute claim.

The same sentence says doc comment string literals do not appear as attributes in syn, but Rust doc comments are represented as `#[doc = "..."]` attributes. The intended behavior can still exclude them, but the ADR should describe that as ignoring `doc` name-value attributes and only matching `doc(hidden)` list forms, not as relying on doc comments being absent from the AST.

T001 is mapped to IN-04/AC-07, which require a reusable syn scanning helper with callback-swappable detection logic, but the implementation plan and infrastructure catalogue only define `doc_hidden::verify` and place file discovery, `syn::parse_file`, cfg-test filtering, and attribute detection inside that detector. That leaves no task/type contract for the common helper the spec requires, so the plan can satisfy the doc-hidden gate while missing the reusable scanning architecture.

The infrastructure catalogue declares no trait implementation changes, but T002 explicitly requires adding `verify_doc_hidden` to `FsVerifyAdapter`'s `VerifyPort` implementation. This makes the type catalogue/contract map inconsistent with the hexagonal wiring plan and can leave the new `VerifyPort` method without a documented infrastructure adapter implementation.

T001 narrows the scanner callback to crate-level attributes plus attributes on `Item` / impl-associated items, but the spec requires failing whenever any non-test source contains a `#[doc(hidden)]`-equivalent attribute. Attributes on other Rust AST nodes such as struct fields, enum variants, and trait associated items are still non-test source attributes and can affect rustdoc output, yet this traversal plan and its tests would not exercise them. As written, the gate can pass with prohibited `#[doc(hidden)]` declarations still present, so AC-02/AC-04 are not actually satisfied.

IN-01 explicitly includes adding the verify gate-name label, but T002 only plans the enum variant, dispatch arm, items_dir arm, and Makefile tasks. In this CLI, verify commands are also matched by a telemetry gate-name function, so following this plan as written leaves the new DocHidden variant without its required gate label and the wiring is incomplete. Add the gate-name label/update to T002 and the rendered plan/type artifacts so IN-01 is actually covered.
